rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference

Compute co-expression network

Source: R/network.R
compute_network.Rd

Calculates correlation, applies normalization (Mutual Rank or CLR), and determines a density-based co-expression threshold. Accepts either a numeric matrix or a SummarizedExperiment.

compute_network(x, ...)

# S4 method for class 'matrix'
compute_network(
  x,
  cor_method = c("pearson", "spearman"),
  norm_method = c("MR", "CLR"),
  density = 0.03,
  abs_cor = FALSE,
  mr_log_transform = FALSE,
  min_var = 0,
  n_cores = 1L,
  use_torch = FALSE
)

# S4 method for class 'SummarizedExperiment'
compute_network(x, assay = 1L, ...)

Arguments

x

Expression data: a numeric matrix (genes x samples) with row names as gene identifiers, or a SummarizedExperiment.

...

Arguments passed to methods (see below).

cor_method

Correlation method: "pearson" (default) or "spearman".

norm_method

Normalization method: "MR" (Mutual Rank, default) or "CLR" (Context Likelihood Ratio).

density

Fraction of top edges to keep (default 0.03 = 3%).

abs_cor

If TRUE, take absolute value of correlations before normalization (default FALSE).

mr_log_transform

If FALSE (default), use the raw MR formula matching the original RComPlEx R Markdown. If TRUE, use Obayashi & Kinoshita (2009) log-normalized formula (values in [0,1]).

min_var

Minimum row variance threshold. Genes with variance below this value are removed before computing correlations. Default 0 removes only constant genes (zero variance). Set to a positive value (e.g. 1e-3) to also remove near-invariant genes that produce noisy correlations. Set to NULL to disable filtering entirely.

n_cores

Number of threads for parallel computation (default 1).

use_torch

If TRUE, use torch for GPU-accelerated correlation (CUDA, MPS, or CPU fallback). Requires the torch package. Default FALSE. On Apple Silicon (MPS), torch uses float32 because Metal does not support float64. Pearson correlation is accurate to ~1e-7, but Spearman + MR normalization can exhibit rank-swap artifacts. For exact Spearman + MR on MPS, keep use_torch = FALSE here and use permutation_hog_test(use_torch = TRUE) for the permutation speedup instead. On CUDA, float64 is used with no precision tradeoff.

assay

Assay name or index to extract from the SummarizedExperiment (default 1). Requires the SummarizedExperiment package.

Value

A list with components:

network

Named symmetric matrix (genes x genes) with normalized co-expression values. Diagonal is set to 0.

threshold

The co-expression threshold at the given density.

n_genes

Number of genes in the network.

n_removed

Number of genes removed by variance filter (0 if min_var is NULL).

params

List of parameters used.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# From a matrix:
net <- compute_network(x, cor_method = "spearman",
                       norm_method = "mr", density = 0.03)

# From a SummarizedExperiment:
net <- compute_network(se, assay = "vst", cor_method = "spearman")
} # }
